channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Swing state official warns village struggling with financial losses after influx of illegal immigrants
item.description: The village of Lockland, Ohio says it is struggling with an influx of migrants from Mauritania, with what it says are dire financial consequences for the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Montana Senate race, which could determine majority, seeing 'intense ground game operation': NRSC Chairman
item.description: NRSC Chairman Steve Daines told Fox News Digital in an exclusive interview he is confident Republicans will secure a Senate majority by flipping the Montana Senate seat this cycle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The last in-person vote in the US will be cast on the desolate tundra of Alaska's Aleutian Islands
item.description: On a desolate slab of island tundra on Alaska's Aleutian Islands, a resident of a tiny village called Adak will again become the last American to cast an in-person ballot for president.
item.dc:creator: Mark Thiessen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: States to election officials: Follow the law or face consequences
item.description: In at least five battleground states, officials have investigated, indicted and even jailed officials who tried to interfere with the vote count.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Harris eyes a rural Maine congressional district in a hunt for every possible electoral vote
item.description: LEWISTON, Maine (AP) — Rob Rogers has seen his rural corner of New England turn into a stronghold for Donald Trump in two consecutive elections. But this year he's feeling hopeful that Vice President Kamala Harris could reclaim a potentially decisive electoral vote for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Five takeaways from a fiery Wisconsin Senate debate
item.description: Sen. Tammy Baldwin (D-Wis.) and Republican challenger Eric Hovde squared off on a debate stage Friday evening in Madison in a fiery exchange of attacks that showed how intense this race has become after largely flying under the radar for months.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taylor Lorenz: Musk draws more political ire than Cuban because of X
item.description: Musk has ardently endorsed Donald Trump, while Mark Cuban and Taylor Swift have thrown support behind Harris.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump couldn't pronounce 'Assyrians.' The community is happy to be in the spotlight
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — It was Donald Trump's mispronunciation that first caught attention. “Also, we have many Asur-Asians in our room,” Trump said at a weekend rally in Prescott Valley, Arizona. “We have some incredible people in our room.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Facing a budget deficit challenge, Johnson aims to safeguard youth and workforce
item.description: Enticing grocery store chains to open in underserved communities is another priority for the Johnson administration.
item.dc:creator: Julian Crews, WGN 9

item.title: Exclusive: Chauncey Parker talks new role as NYC deputy mayor for public safety
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams on Tuesday appointed Chauncey Parker to be New York City’s new deputy mayor for public safety. Parker takes over the position that was held by Philip Banks, who resigned the week prior.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Judge unseals heavily redacted trove of evidence in Trump's 2020 election interference case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The judge overseeing Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case made public Friday a heavily redacted trove of documents that provide a small glimpse into the evidence prosecutors will present if the case ever goes to trial.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP lawmakers seek investigation of consulting firm over its work in China
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A group of Republican lawmakers is demanding a federal investigation into the U.S. consulting firm McKinsey over its work with the Chinese government and state-owned enterprises, even as the company reportedly is revamping its China business to reduce risks.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump hotel attracted would-be judges, ambassadors, pardon-seekers, House Democrats say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Judges seeking appointments to the federal bench. Wealthy Republicans hoping for ambassadorships. Criminals who wanted pardons. They were among the big spenders at Donald Trump ’s Washington, D.C., hotel while he was president.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Election 2024: How the 'New York Seven' could decide the House
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York voters have much to consider on Election Day. In addition to voting for the next president of the United States, New Yorkers will be asked to decide on a proposed amendment to the state Constitution, a U.S. Senate seat, and House representatives.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Bret Baier says he made 'a mistake' during Harris interview
item.description: Fox News anchor Bret Baier said former President Donald Trump's comments about "the enemy within" was mistakenly absent from a clip shown.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: What's a 'Jezebel spirit'? Some Christians use the term to paint Kamala Harris with a demonic brush
item.description: Christian nationalist leaders are telling followers that Vice President Kamala Harris is under the influence of a “Jezebel spirit,” using a term with deeply racist and misogynistic roots that is setting off alarm bells for religious and political scholars.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT and MICHELLE R. SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politics Friday: The 2024 election will determine who presides in Minnesota courtrooms, remember there are judges on your ballot
item.description: There are about 100 races on Minnesota’s election ballots that won’t get that much attention, and they’re all about judgeships. MPR News politics editor and his guests talk about judicial elections and why they’re different here than in other states.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Matthew Alvarez, and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Georgia Republicans readying post-election legal strategy
item.description: Georgia GOP Chairman Josh McKoon spoke with Fox News Digital about his party's legal strategy both before and after Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chutkan unseals much-redacted Jack Smith evidence used to build Trump's Jan. 6 case
item.description: A judge on Friday unsealed nearly 1,900 pages of evidence special counsel Jack Smith assembled in building the election interference case against former President Trump, publicly posting the highly redacted trove.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Republicans mainstreamed the baseless idea of noncitizen voting in 2024
item.description: Donald Trump and his allies have zeroed in on the baseless claim that Democrats are encouraging newly arrived migrants to vote for them in the 2024 election. There is no evidence of a plot like this.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block and Miles Parks, MPR News

item.title: Following death of Hamas leader, Harris says it's 'time for the day after to begin' in Gaza
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Kamala Harris said Thursday that Israel's killing of Hamas' top leader offers "an opportunity to finally end the war in Gaza," talking plainly about next steps even as the pro-Palestinian demonstrators who gathered outside her campaign event underscored the complicated politics at p
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, COLLEEN LONG and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Almost 23% of people say politics influences where they live: Poll
item.description: "Some go so far as to consider moving to find that alignment," Danielle Hale, chief economist for Realtor.com, said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump visits barbershop in the Bronx
item.description: THE BRONX, N.Y. (PIX11) – Former President Donald Trump visited a barbershop in the Bronx on Thursday. Trump stopped by Javiel's Barbershop on Castle Hill Avenue.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: McConnell privately slammed Trump as ‘stupid’ and ‘despicable’ after 2020 election
item.description: McConnell also criticized the former president as a “narcissist” and admitted after the 2020 election that “it’s not just the Democrats who are counting the days” until he left office.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Harris, Trump trade insults over who is fit to serve
item.description: The question of each presidential candidate's fitness to serve has become a breeding ground for insults between Vice President Harris and former President Trump, who are trying to paint each other as incapable of being the next commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: GOP cringes over Trump’s vow to wield military against opponents
item.description: Former President Trump’s incendiary comments about deploying the military and using other powers of the executive branch to pursue his political enemies, including a sitting member of Congress, is putting downballot Republican candidates on the defensive.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Harris, Bret Baier spar over immigration at outset of Fox News interview
item.description: Vice President Harris on Wednesday sparred at length with Fox News anchor Bret Baier over immigration in a rare sit-down with the network. Baier opened the interview by asking Harris how many immigrants had crossed the southern border during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: How to watch the Nevada US Senate Debate
item.description: The only debate between Democratic incumbent Sen. Jacky Rosen and Republican challenger Sam Brown will occur on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden administration forgives student loans for 60,000 public service workers
item.description: The Biden administration announced Thursday that it is forgiving the student loans for more than 60,000 public service workers. The move gives $4.5 billion in debt relief to teachers, firefighters and other eligible workers who have been paying on their loans for more than 10 years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tried a friendlier pitch with Latino voters at a Univision town hall
item.description: During a town hall outside Miami, Trump touted his record on the economy, yet called Jan. 6 a "day of love" and would not back off false claims about Haitian migrants eating pets in Springfield, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Harris Fox News interview draws sharp reactions on both sides
item.description: “If she wins, it will be because of that answer … which is ‘I’m not Joe Biden,’” columnist Chris Cillizza noted.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Ag leaders voice concerns over negative impacts of tariffs proposed in presidential election
item.description: Midwestern agricultural leaders say they are concerned new tariffs being suggested by former President Donald Trump as part of his reelection bid could result in a damaging trade war. They say it could cost the corn and soybeans billions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Biden to travel to Germany Thursday, delays African leg of trip
item.description: In Berlin, Biden will convene a meeting with German Chancellor Olaf Scholz to discuss continued support for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Lyft launches voter transportation initiative
item.description: “Lyft believes transportation access should never be a barrier for any citizen seeking to vote," Lyft Chief Policy Officer Jerry Golden said in the release.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Man arrested near Trump Coachella rally with gun sues for defamation
item.description: The man arrested near former President Trump’s Coachella, Calif., rally over the weekend sued the Riverside County sheriff for defamation and other claims, saying there were “preposterous allegations” made about the incident possibly being an assassination attempt.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk has given $75 million, so far, to put Donald Trump back in the White House
item.description: Elon Musk is among the largest financial supporters of Donald Trump's reelection efforts, new FEC filings show. He and two other megadonors collectively gave $300 million to support Trump.
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel, MPR News

item.title: Johnson: Additional hurricane aid 'can wait' until Congress is back in session
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Sunday said passing additional hurricane aid for states impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton “can wait” until Congress is back in session after the election.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC
